nodes.label: Restaurant Wait Time Predictor — M1 Reference PDF (References)
nodes.source_file: Stock-Market-Trend-Analysis/References/Milestone 1_ Data Collection & Preprocessing · Restaurant Wait Time Predictor.pdf
nodes.norm_label: restaurant wait time predictor — m1 reference pdf (references)

nodes.label: Efficient-Market Hypothesis (EMH) Ceiling
nodes.rationale: The headline null result is treated as the deliverable: no economically usable out-of-sample skill. Next-day mean is near-unpredictable while next-day variance is strongly predictable; even that variance-derived directional edge is erased by transaction costs — textbook EMH evidence.

nodes.label: Leakage-Prevention Invariants
nodes.rationale: The whole project rests on six guards: next-day target built per split, shift(1)-before-rolling on all rolling/lag features, expanding past-only target encoding, fit-on-train-only scaler/models, holdout opened exactly once, and ticker-scoped exogenous ffill. Agent audits caught real leaks (off-by-one target, global-mean encoding).

nodes.label: Next-Day Target (target_log_return)
nodes.rationale: groupby(ticker).log_return.shift(-1) built per split so the last row of each split per ticker is NaN — same-day log_return is a predictor, not the label. Fixes a BLOCKER off-by-one leak flagged by the Model QA agent.

nodes.label: Log-Return Stationarity (ADF)
nodes.rationale: ADF confirms Adj Close is non-stationary (p>=0.86) but log_return is stationary in mean (p=0.0000), so ARIMA can fit returns with d=0. Standard finance practice; forecast log return, not Close.

nodes.label: Chronological Time-Based Split
nodes.rationale: train<=2023-12-29 (7,660), val=2024 (1,008), holdout=2025+ (1,384). A random split would leak the future; holdout reserves an unbiased final test opened exactly once.

nodes.label: Volatility Clustering (Conditional Heteroskedasticity)
nodes.rationale: ARCH-LM p~0 and ACF(r^2) lag-1 up to 0.487 for every ticker: returns are stationary in mean but conditionally heteroskedastic. Next-day variance is strongly predictable — the single most important result for model selection, motivating a GARCH variance model.

nodes.label: Leverage Effect (Asymmetric Volatility)
nodes.rationale: Down days raise next-day volatility more than up days (significant all 4 tickers, p 7e-6..0.035), so a symmetric GARCH(1,1) would miss the asymmetry — recommend EGARCH/GJR-GARCH (o=1).

nodes.label: Mutual-Information Feature Ranking
nodes.rationale: Pearson is ~0 for every feature and hid the signal; MI shows volatility features carry ~67x the shuffle noise floor (parkinson_vol_21 MI=0.16 vs Pearson ~0.01). This nonlinear vol-based structure is the quantitative justification for a nonlinear LSTM.

nodes.label: ARIMA (per-ticker, BIC order)
nodes.rationale: Order by BIC (parsimonious (1,0,0)/(0,0,1)) because AIC over-selected (2,0,2); causal append-then-forecast one-step after an off-by-one BLOCKER fix. Ties naive on holdout RMSE (DM p=0.78), directional coin flip (0.514).

nodes.label: GJR-GARCH(1,1) Student-t
nodes.rationale: Asymmetric o=1 (chosen because M2 proved the leverage effect), Student-t errors for fat tails (nu~5), evaluated as a variance model via QLIKE + Mincer-Zarnowitz not point RMSE. Its mean forecast is the one out-of-sample survivor (DirAcc 54.3%, binomial p=0.002) but is uneconomic after transaction costs. Originally mislabeled EGARCH; corrected by audit.

nodes.label: Global Attention LSTM
nodes.rationale: Keras functional model, 60-day sequences + ticker one-hot, Bahdanau-style additive attention over timesteps, StandardScaler train-only, shuffle=False. Only a 2-epoch CPU smoke-test locally (RMSE 0.132 is plumbing, not a result); full training pending Colab GPU.

nodes.label: LightGBM (tabular, exogenous)
nodes.rationale: 39 features + ticker categorical, deliberately un-tuned so the overfit is the lesson. 8/15 top importances are exogenous and it lifts val DirAcc to 0.539 (p=0.015), but holdout RMSE (0.02165) is significantly WORSE than naive (DM p=0.003) and direction is a coin flip (0.499).

nodes.label: Exogenous Features (VIX/Yields/Dollar)
nodes.rationale: Nine leakage-safe macro features (vix_level, vix_log_change, vix_z_60, is_high_vol_regime, tnx_level, tnx_change, term_spread, term_spread_change, dxy_log_return) — the only break from price-history-only, all market-priced daily and known at close t (no CPI release-lag). Same-day VIX corr -0.70 vs next-day +0.05 proves no label contamination. MODEL_FEATURES 30->39.

nodes.label: Directional Significance Testing (Binomial + Diebold-Mariano)
nodes.rationale: Added because a 55% DirAcc was read as skill without a test. Binomial test (DirAcc vs 0.50) + Diebold-Mariano (RMSE vs naive) on val and holdout exposed the val->holdout skill decay (GARCH val 0.555 p=0.0006 shrinks to 0.543 p=0.002).

nodes.label: MODEL_FEATURES Contract (feature_roles.json)
nodes.rationale: Machine-readable role separation (model_features, target, raw_price_level_excluded, diagnostic_cols, exog_raw). Raw non-stationary price levels (close_lag_*, sma_*) are kept but excluded from X in favor of stationary substitutes (momentum, price_to_sma). M3 trains on MODEL_FEATURES only and dropna(subset=MODEL_FEATURES).

nodes.label: Multi-Agent Adversarial Audit
nodes.rationale: Each milestone is reviewed in parallel by independent expert subagents (Investment Researcher, Model QA Specialist, Data Engineer on M1) who reproduce every headline number before flagging by severity. They caught real BLOCKERs: next-day off-by-one target, global-mean encoding leakage, vacuous sealing check, ARIMA one-step off-by-one, EGARCH mislabel.

nodes.label: Milestone-Driven Project Pattern (Mhmd-Ram/DS-Project)
nodes.rationale: Structure adapted from Mhmd-Ram/DS-Project: a milestone-driven, AI-executable capstone scaffold (spec in Plans/, report in reports/milestones/, append-only Decisions Log) chosen for a consistent, cheap-model-executable workflow across four milestones.
